threat_model: Readiness waiver review can become a policy bypass if waiver grant, deployment mutation, production promotion, accepted-risk expiry, rollback evidence, and incident handoff are conflated. The main risks are treating missing evidence as approval, allowing deployment mutation from a review packet, serializing secret material, using a waiver as terminal closure, or claiming readiness success from compensating controls alone. The mitigation is a non-executing packet schema and validator that require expiry, approval evidence, accepted-risk evidence, rollback/recovery, incident handoff, blocked reason refs, and explicit denial flags in Foundation Mode.

required_checks.check: waiver authority remains false in Foundation example
deployment witness boundary confirms mutation and production promotion remain denied
redaction check confirms raw secret material remains denied
policy bypass check confirms waiver cannot bypass Phi_gov, operator approval, accepted-risk expiry, rollback, or incident handoff
receipt integrity check confirms required evidence and blocked reason refs are retained
expiry and compensating control drift are rejected
audit visibility check confirms proof coverage matrix includes the waiver review surface

findings.mitigation: Foundation Mode packet blocks waiver grant, deployment mutation, production promotion, terminal closure, and readiness success claims until named evidence refs exist.

mitigations: keep ReadinessWaiverReviewPacket validation non-live and deterministic
require Phi_gov and operator approval refs before waiver grant can be reconsidered
require accepted-risk expiry evidence before any temporary waiver
require rollback and incident handoff evidence before deployment mutation
reject raw secret material and readiness success claims in Foundation Mode
